paper_title: Glacier Mass Balance and ELA Sensitivity Analysis

methods.description: A simple glacier mass balance model was applied to explore regional differences in the Equilibrium Line Altitude (ELA) between the Mid-Holocene (MH) and Pre-Industrial (PI) periods in the southern mid-latitudes. The model calculates mass balance gradients based on daily temperature and precipitation data as a function of elevation.

steps.description: Obtain daily temperature and precipitation data from PMIP2 simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch daily temperature and precipitation data from PMIP2 simulations

steps.description: Calculate mass balance for each elevation using daily temperature and precipitation data.
steps.searchable_summary: compute mass balance using temperature and precipitation data

steps.description: Define accumulation as the portion of daily precipitation that falls as snow when the daily average temperature is below a certain threshold (T crit).
steps.searchable_summary: define snow accumulation based on temperature threshold

steps.description: Calculate ablation using mean daily temperature when it is positive, using a degree day factor (DDF).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ablation using positive daily temperature and DDF

steps.description: Validate model results by comparing with existing glacier mass balance data from Mocho Glacier.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model results against Mocho Glacier data

methods.description: Use daily GCM temperature and precipitation values from PMIP2 simulations to drive the glacier mass balance model. Compare PI outputs with gridded temperature and precipitation data from CRU TS3.10 and weather station observations.

steps.description: Fetch daily GCM temperature and precipitation values from PMIP2 simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve daily GCM data from PMIP2

steps.description: Compare PI outputs with CRU TS3.10 gridded data and weather station observations.
steps.searchable_summary: compare PI outputs with CRU TS3.10 and weather station data

methods.description: Perform sensitivity analysis to assess the robustness of the model results by varying precipitation lapse rate and degree day factor (DDF) for snow and ice.

steps.description: Assess sensitivity of ELA to precipitation lapse rate values of 0, 0.001, and 0.02 mm m−1.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate ELA sensitivity to precipitation lapse rate

steps.description: Assess sensitivity of ELA to DDF values of 4, 8, and 10 mm d−1 °C−1 for ice and 2 and 4 mm d−1 °C−1 for snow.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate ELA sensitivity to DDF values
